note: Jerry Colonna explains the word 'complicit' by comparing it to being an accomplice: 'You are driving the getaway car, you're not sticking up the bank teller.' The distinction matters — complicit doesn't mean responsible. It means you played a part without realizing it.
sourceTitle: How have I been complicit in creating the conditions I say I don't want? | Jerry Colonna

lyricText: I say I don't want the noise
note: Jerry gives this exact example on the podcast: 'I say I don't want to feel busy all the time, but the truth of the matter is I feel really unnerved and disconcerted if my agenda isn't jam-packed.' The complicit question reveals the gap between what we claim to want and what we actually choose.
sourceTitle: How have I been complicit in creating the conditions I say I don't want? | Jerry Colonna

note: In his book Reboot, Jerry describes finding a toppled oak tree in Marin County and saying to himself, 'Here lies a good man.' He imagines the tree lived 75-80 years with limbs gnarled by good and bad choices, but spent its life providing shelter and shade — slowly dissolving into the earth, having lived its purpose.
sourceTitle: How have I been complicit in creating the conditions I say I don't want? | Jerry Colonna

note: Jerry quotes Carl Jung on the podcast: 'Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.' He applies this to teams — unconscious patterns repeat until someone names them, and the whole group becomes a manifestation of the leader's unexamined dysfunction.
sourceTitle: How have I been complicit in creating the conditions I say I don't want? | Jerry Colonna

note: The subtitle of Jerry's first book is 'Leadership and the Art of Growing Up.' On the podcast he says: 'The Art of Growing Up is a practice. It's not a scientific moment where one day you wake up and you're done. It's an ongoing practice of continuous inquiry that can feel exhausting when you contemplate it, but enlivening when you live it.'
sourceTitle: How have I been complicit in creating the conditions I say I don't want? | Jerry Colonna
